svg.text: SoC — Memory Architecture & ROM Latency Path
top_hdmi_gb · gb_rom_ddr_bridge · lpddr2_rom_ctrl_128_fifo · lpddr2_emif (UniPHY) — low-latency local memory shields the cycle-accurate core from DDR latency
if a ROM wait hangs too long
sanity check vs raw byte stream
held in reset until ADV7513 ready
ZERO wait — SRAM fast path (<512 KiB),
ONE cycle — local BRAM read, SoC-cache
TIMING-SENSITIVE REGIONS — must not depend on external DDR latency

• Boot ROM + cartridge bank 0/1 (incl. the 0x0100–0x01FF Nintendo-logo fetch): served from zero-wait async SRAM (originally local BRAM) so the cycle-accurate boot never waits on the high-latency DDR bridge.

• Any ROM read < 512 KiB and every SoC line-cache hit return with no wait state, preserving exact CPU/PPU timing. Only a genuine LPDDR2 line miss asserts io_stall, which freezes the whole core in lock-step (bounded by a watchdog).

• Producer→consumer CDC — framebuffer double-buffer into pix_clk (vblank-gated swap) and APU samples into aud_mclk (req/ack toggle, startup-muted) — keeps variable DDR/SD timing from causing tearing or audio phase drift.
• SD ROM loading runs entirely in ddr_afi_clk and is fenced off from the core by the sticky rst_soc: the CPU is only released once rom_done & cal_success, so no half-loaded ROM is ever fetched.
